channel.title: Notes on palucki
channel.description: Recent content in Notes on palucki

item.title: Git tips & tricks
item.description: Table of contents Fetch branch (not current) from remote repo (prepare for rebase): Fetch branch (not current) from remote repo (prepare for rebase): git fetch origin release:release Force push only your current branch: git push origin +youBranchName Rename local and remote branch # Rename branch locally git branch -m old_branch new_branch # Delete the old branch git push origin :old_branch # Push the new branch, set local branch to track the new remote git push --set-upstream origin new_branch Rebase onto Providing we have:

item.title: Refactoring legacy code
item.description: Note: below you can find my jottings based on Kate Gregory course Table of contents Introduction How much time dedicate to refactoring? Refactoring process Use compiler - it’s you friend Use correct C++ as a C++ Use new C++ features Use standard library Reorganizing code Introduction Refactoring legacy code generally means transforming code to be: more expressive, more readable, simpler.
